pages.text: The $(item)Aquatic Skyseed$() — clay and sand. Both come from a $(bold)Desert island$(): clay in its core, sand on top.
Everything's in hand — here's how it goes together.

Half land, half water. An irregular pool sits in its centre — never a perfect circle — fed with whatever grows in the water it lands over. Thrown over a $(item)river$() it instead cuts a winding channel clean across the island.

$(bold)Waters$()$(br)Freshwater grows $(item)kelp$(), $(item)seagrass$(), and $(item)lily pads$(); over an ocean, a small reef of $(item)coral$(), sea pickles and seagrass. $(item)Squid$() swim the freshwater, $(item)tropical fish$() the reef, and $(item)salmon$() a river.
$(bold)Mud & banks$()$(br)Over a swamp or mangrove it grows $(item)mud$() and mangroves. The bed is thick with $(item)clay$() and gravel — the point of the island.

$(bold)Sunken stone$()$(br)Now and then the pool comes up an $(o)ocean ruin$() instead — a flooded stone-brick basin. Brush its $(item)suspicious sand$() for archaeology finds (a $(item)sniffer egg$(), if you're lucky) and loot the sunken chest.
